evals.prompt:
Draft a SOC 2 Type II-shaped controls report (a DRAFT for internal readiness, NOT an issued attestation) from these survived findings. Topic: 'access-control and change-management controls for a SaaS platform'. Include all required sections and a tests-of-controls matrix. Write the report to compliance-audit.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_soc_1 | title: Multi-factor authentication is enforced for all administrative console access via the identity provider | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/mfa-control-evidence | author: Internal Security | year: 2026
- id: urn:mif:f_soc_2 | title: All production code changes require peer review and pass automated CI gates before merge | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/change-management-evidence | author: Engineering | year: 2026
- id: urn:mif:f_soc_3 | title: Encrypted backups of customer data are taken daily and restoration is tested quarterly | dimension: availability | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/backup-restore-evidence | author: Platform Ops | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: A SOC 2 Type II-shaped controls report DRAFT with, in order: Independent Service Auditor's Report (framed as a draft expressing NO opinion), Management's Assertion, System Description (infrastructure/software/people/data/processes), Trust Services Criteria (Security plus Availability), Tests of Controls & Results (a matrix of control / test performed / result or exception), and Other Information. A strongly-stated caveat that this is not an attestation, assurance, or audit opinion by a licensed CPA firm. No raw urn:mif: identifiers in prose.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Required Independent Service Auditor's Report section present
Required Management's Assertion section present
Required System Description section present
Required Trust Services Criteria section present
Security (common criteria) Trust Services Criterion named
Required Tests of Controls & Results section present
Controls/test-results matrix has a markdown table header separator row
Matrix references controls
Matrix reports test/result/exception
Strongly-stated no-attestation / no-assurance / no-opinion caveat present
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose or body text
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose or body text
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_soc_' must not appear as visible body text. Internal MIF IDs are resolved to citations, never exposed to the reader.

expectations.description: The report explicitly disclaims attestation, assurance, and audit opinion
expectations.criteria: The report states plainly that it is a draft/model, that no licensed CPA firm performed an examination, and that it does not constitute an attestation, assurance, certification, or audit opinion. The Independent Service Auditor's Report section is framed as a placeholder expressing no opinion.

evals.prompt:
Produce a SOC 2 Type II-shaped controls report DRAFT on 'confidentiality and processing-integrity controls for a payments service'. The finding set includes one falsified finding and one weakened finding — handle each according to the genre rules. Include the tests-of-controls matrix. Write the report to compliance-audit.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_pay_1 | title: Customer payment data is encrypted at rest with AES-256 and in transit with TLS 1.3 | dimension: confidentiality | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/encryption-control | author: Security | year: 2026
- id: urn:mif:f_pay_2 | title: Every reconciliation run reaches 100% transaction completeness with zero unreconciled records across all periods | dimension: processing-integrity | verdict: falsified | citation: https://example.com/reconciliation-claim | author: Finance Eng | year: 2026
- id: urn:mif:f_pay_3 | title: Input validation rejects malformed transaction payloads before processing, with an estimated 3x reduction in downstream errors | dimension: processing-integrity | verdict: weakened | citation: https://example.com/input-validation-study | author: Platform | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: A controls report DRAFT that: (1) excludes the falsified finding (f_pay_2 / 100% reconciliation claim — must not appear as a tested control), (2) includes the weakened finding (f_pay_3) but annotates it as weakened/uncertain, (3) includes the survived finding (f_pay_1) as evidence, (4) maintains all six required sections including Trust Services Criteria and a tests-of-controls matrix, (5) carries the strong no-attestation caveat.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Management's Assertion section present
System Description section present
Trust Services Criteria section present
In-scope criterion (Confidentiality or Processing Integrity) named
Tests of Controls & Results section present
Controls/test-results matrix has a markdown table header separator row
Survived finding f_pay_1 (encryption control) appears in the report
Weakened finding f_pay_3 (input validation) appears in the report (weakened = include with annotation, not exclude)
Strongly-stated no-attestation / no-assurance / no-opinion caveat present
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: The falsified finding (100% reconciliation completeness) does NOT appear as a tested control
expectations.criteria: The claim of 100% reconciliation completeness with zero unreconciled records must not appear as a control or supported result. The falsified finding may only be mentioned in a methodology note describing the exclusion policy — never as evidence.

expectations.description: The weakened finding (3x error reduction) is presented with an explicit uncertainty qualifier
expectations.criteria: The input-validation finding must carry an inline qualifier such as 'weakened evidence', 'preliminary', 'contested', or 'estimated' and must not be stated with the same confidence as the survived encryption control.

expectations.description: The report carries the strong no-attestation caveat
expectations.criteria: The report states it is a draft/model and not an attestation, assurance, certification, or audit opinion by a licensed CPA firm.

evals.prompt:
Draft a SOC 2 Type II-shaped controls report from these findings. Topic: 'security and availability controls for a managed database service'. All findings survived falsification. Every control row in the tests-of-controls matrix must trace to a finding. Write the report to compliance-audit.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_db_1 | title: Network access to database instances is restricted by security groups allowing only application-tier traffic | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/network-isolation | author: Infra | year: 2026
- id: urn:mif:f_db_2 | title: Vulnerability scans run weekly and critical findings are remediated within a 7-day SLA | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/vuln-mgmt | author: Security | year: 2026
- id: urn:mif:f_db_3 | title: The service maintains 99.95% measured monthly uptime with automated failover across availability zones | dimension: availability | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/uptime-evidence | author: SRE | year: 2026
- id: urn:mif:f_db_4 | title: Access to production databases is logged and reviewed monthly, with privileged sessions recorded | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/access-logging | author: Security | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: A complete SOC 2-shaped controls report DRAFT with all six sections. The Trust Services Criteria section names Security and Availability. The tests-of-controls matrix has one row per control tracing to a supplied finding. The no-attestation caveat is present. No urn:mif: leakage in prose.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Independent Service Auditor's Report section present
System Description section present
Trust Services Criteria section present
Security criterion named
Availability criterion named
Other Information section present
Tests of Controls & Results section present
Controls/test-results matrix has a markdown table header separator row
Finding f_db_1 (network isolation) referenced
Finding f_db_2 (vulnerability management) referenced
Finding f_db_3 (uptime/failover) referenced
Finding f_db_4 (access logging) referenced
Strongly-stated no-attestation caveat present
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the body text
No internal MIF finding ID handles leak into the output body

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or f_db_N internal IDs appear in the body text
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_db_' must not appear as visible prose. All references resolve to citations.

expectations.description: Every control row in the matrix traces to one of the four supplied findings
expectations.criteria: The tests-of-controls matrix contains a control row for each of the four findings (network isolation, vulnerability management, uptime/failover, access logging), each with a test performed and a result/exception, and no orphan control lacking a cited finding.

expectations.description: The report carries the strong no-attestation caveat
expectations.criteria: The report states it is a draft/model and not an attestation, assurance, certification, or audit opinion by a licensed CPA firm.
